meta.title: ERC EDW ingress — seven-approach figures with license vs infrastructure split
meta.purpose: Machine-readable source of truth for the full 7-approach comparison. License, infrastructure, and labor are distinct streams, each with capex (one-time) and monthly (recurring). 3-yr TCO = capex + 36 x monthly.
meta.cost_basis: Cloud: live Azure Retail (West US, 2026-07-22) + published OCI list. License list prices 2026 (Oracle DB EE $47,500/proc, SE2 ~$17,500/socket, +22%/yr support; SQL 2022 Std $3,944 / Ent $15,122 per 2-core pack, +~25%/yr SA; OIC ~$943/mo/pack; ADW OCPU $4.0288 Lic-Incl / $1.3441 BYOL). On-prem hardware/labor are documented planning estimates.
meta.caveat: Planning estimates; not a quote. Enterprise license discounts (50-80% off list) and cloud reservations (30-41% off) are NOT applied. Blended labor $150/hr. Model: seven_approach_cost_model.py.

5b_oci_byol.label: OCI-native (BYOL)
5b_oci_byol.note: ADW at BYOL rate; license = OIC only; assumes owned Oracle DB licenses

6b_onprem_oracle_ee.label: On-prem Oracle EE
6b_onprem_oracle_ee.note: EE license alone (~$315k/3yr) exceeds the entire ADF TCO

key_findings: License is an explicit separable SKU on-prem and in Oracle OCI; hyperscalers bundle it into the service rate.
Fabric is ~41% license (capacity subscription); Databricks DBU is license on rented infra; roll-your-own is only 2% license (traded for 90% labor).
On-prem TCO (~$506k) is dominated by infrastructure (hardware ~$210k/3yr) + labor (~$162k/3yr), not license — for Standard/SE2 tiers.
Oracle Enterprise Edition license alone (~$315k/3yr) exceeds the entire Azure-managed ADF TCO (~$159k).
OCI-native BYOL (~$233k) + zero cross-cloud egress is the strongest single-vendor/data-gravity play.
Ranking by 3-yr TCO: ADF $159k < Fabric $203k < Databricks $231k < OCI-native $256k < Roll-your-own $388k < On-prem SE2/SQL ~$506k < Oracle EE $763k.
